  "decision": "fail",

  "findings": [
    "Isolated metal parts not bonded to the equipment grounding conductor.",
    "Potential difference between unbonded metal parts and ground creates shock hazard.",
    "Missing bonding jumpers or improper termination observed on isolated metallic components."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately secure the work area and de-energize affected equipment.",
    "Install listed bonding jumpers between all isolated metal parts and the equipment grounding conductor per NEC 250.104.",
    "Verify continuity and impedance of bonding path before re-energizing.",
    "Inspect all bonding connections for mechanical security and corrosion resistance."
  ],
  "rationale": "During in-progress electrical work on equipment bonding, isolated metal parts that are not bonded present a high risk of electric shock and arc flash. NEC 250.104 requires effective bonding of non-current-carrying metal parts likely to become energized. The absence of bonding is a non-compliant and potentially installed-defective condition that must be corrected before work continues.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 250.104",
    "NEC 250.30"
  ]
}
